name: Bug fix: GitHub Pages viewer must publish split memory graph refs
summary: Bug fix: GitHub Pages viewer must publish split memory graph refs. The hosted viewer loads data/kage/graph.json, and compact memory graphs reference entities.json, edges.json, and episodes.json beside graph.json. Pages m

name: Bug fix: inspector connected relation sections need their own scroll containers
summary: Bug fix: inspector connected relation sections need their own scroll containers. Bounding only the overall inspector still leaves high degree nodes with very long Connected Relations and Memory Code Evidence content. det

name: Bug fix: the viewer inspector could explode the page height when a selected node had long
summary: Bug fix: the viewer inspector could explode the page height when a selected node had long detail rows or many connected relation summaries, which made the canvas appear to jump or disappear. Keep the details panel as a b

name: Bug fix: viewer memory-code graphs looked disconnected because knowledge graph generation
summary: Bug fix: viewer memory code graphs looked disconnected because knowledge graph generation created broad symbol/test edges from packet paths and viewer canonicalization let stale symbol/test path aliases fall back to file

name: Command-intent recall must prefer runbooks over release proof notes
summary: Fact: Command style queries such as how do I run tests must boost runbook and repo map packets and mildly penalize release/proof decisions that only mention verification commands. Why: BM25 alone can rank a release note

name: Hosted viewer must hydrate compact memory graph
summary: The hosted GitHub Pages viewer must load ./data/kage/graph.json through loadGraphPath, not fetchJson. The published graph is compact and stores memory entities/edges/episodes in refs; raw fetchJson leaves those refs unre

name: Large repo benchmark exposed stale read and exact symbol ranking fixes
summary: Benchmarking synthetic 5.5k and 20k file repos showed structural indexing works at full coverage, but also exposed two issues: read only code graph queries trusted the old structural manifest after source edits, and natu

name: Memory-code relation filter excludes path-only edges
summary: The viewer relation filter previously treated affects path as a memory code relation, but raw affects path usually connects a memory packet to a memory graph path node rather than to a code node. This made the Memory Cod

name: Release 1.1.16 retries npm registry verification
summary: Release 1.1.16 fixes the guarded npm release helper after 1.1.15 exposed npm registry propagation lag: npm publish returned success, but immediate exact version npm view still returned 404. The release helper now gives t

name: Structured memory parser must preserve dotted commands and explicit decisions
summary: Fact: Inline structured memory labels must be parsed up to the next known label instead of the next period. Why: Verification commands often contain dotted paths such as mcp/dist/cli.js, and truncating at a period loses

name: Viewer bridges path-level memory to code files
summary: Path level memory such as affects path: src can leave combined viewer graphs looking disconnected from actual code files because path nodes do not canonicalize to file:src/... code nodes. The viewer should add capped vir

name: Viewer combined mode must prioritize visible memory-code links
summary: The combined viewer can still look weak after memory code bridge support if the visible edge cap spends too much budget on code code and memory only relations. In default combined signal mode, cap the canvas edge count b

name: Viewer must not map file path aliases to arbitrary symbols
summary: Viewer merge aliases must not let a bare file path such as mcp/kernel.ts canonicalize to an arbitrary symbol in that file. Symbol and route code nodes should not advertise their file path as an alias; file paths belong t

name: Viewer node inspector shows type-specific code details
summary: Kage viewer selected node details should expose type specific code metadata and adjacent relations, not just generic ID/summary rows. Code file nodes should show path, language, parser, size, line count, and hash; symbol

name: Viewer signal mode must preserve code graph endpoints
summary: Large code graphs can have valid code relations but show zero visible relations if viewer signal ranking keeps only high degree symbols/tests and drops file endpoints. In code view, signal selection should add connected

name: 3D viewer orb keeps 2D-style interactions
summary: The 3D viewer should read as an orb, not a tilted copy of the 2D force layout. It uses deterministic spherical positions and circular sprite nodes styled with the same nodeThemeColor/nodeFillColor rules as the 2D canvas.

name: 3D viewer physics and edge visibility
summary: The 3D viewer uses a bounded orb preserving force relaxation, not a full unbounded force graph. It starts from deterministic spherical positions, then applies node repulsion, edge spring forces, radial orb gravity, and a

name: Viewer 3D graph mode architecture
summary: The viewer keeps 2D canvas as the default graph renderer and adds an optional Graph Mode=3D Space path. 3D reuses the same visible entity/edge filtering and selection state, lazy loads Three.js from /vendor/three with a

name: Audit trust scoring must not let dense graph edges mask missing context
summary: Fact: Kage audit now reports structured engineering memory coverage, code graph precision, memory code evidence, and a trust score. Why: Dense memory code graph edges can make a repository look well covered even when app

name: Code graph reuses per-file facts by content hash
summary: Kage code graph generation should cache per file extracted facts under .agent memory/code graph/file cache keyed by the file content hash. Unchanged files reuse cached file metadata, symbols, and imports; changed files m

name: Code index, inbox, and benchmark gates harden Kage handoff quality
summary: Fact: Kage now has a first class memory inbox report, an LSP compatible code index command, CI/PR/sync workflow steps that generate that index before refresh, and benchmark gates with an overall score. Why: Audit and PR

name: Code index prefers SCIP with LSP fallback
summary: Kage code index should prefer the external SCIP path for JS/TS repos when scip typescript and the scip CLI are installed, convert the generated index into .agent memory/code index/scip.json, and fall back to the built in

name: Codex demo should show recall, code graph, and activation proof
summary: When demonstrating Kage with Codex, show three concrete steps: kage recall for repo memory, kage code graph for source derived context, and kage setup verify agent for activation proof. If verify agent reports mcp tool r

name: Decision: canvas graph must stay in Kage terminal theme
summary: Decision: Kage's canvas force graph should keep the Kage terminal visual language. Use a restrained near black shell background, phosphor green for memory, cyan for code, amber for tests/review, red only for risk, and mu

name: Decision: Kage setup must verify active agent reachability, not just write config
summary: Decision: Kage setup must verify active agent reachability, not just write config. CLI verification can confirm config, policy, indexes, recall, and code graph, but only MCP kage verify agent proves the current agent ses

name: Decision: Kage viewer uses canvas force graph for interactive exploration
summary: Decision: Kage viewer should use a custom canvas force graph for the primary graph surface. Keep Kage's high signal filtering and repo/code/memory semantics, but render visible nodes with force simulation, drag, pan, whe

name: Decision: repo-local Kage memory should be written directly as git-visible packets; human
summary: Decision: repo local Kage memory should be written directly as git visible packets; human review is required only when promoting memory to org, global, public, registry, or CDN scopes.

name: Decision: viewer hides raw full graph scopes
summary: Decision: Kage viewer should not expose raw Focus selection or Everything graph scopes as normal product controls. Keep memory code edges internally for recall, ranking, stale checks, and evidence, but show them in the i

name: Decision: viewer must default to high-signal graph
summary: Decision: Kage viewer should not render every code and memory node by default. Dependency paths, node modules, generated graph artifacts, and lockfile noise are hidden unless the user opts into dependency noise; the grap

name: Decision: viewer search accepts natural-language graph queries
summary: Decision: the viewer search bar should behave like lightweight graph search, not exact substring search. Natural language questions such as 'Where is the memory about running tests?' are tokenized, stop words are removed

name: gbrain OSS case study documents Kage-assisted issue sprint
summary: The gbrain OSS sprint should be documented as a separate case study, not bloated into the README. Keep README and website links concise, and put the detailed proof in docs/OSS GBRAIN CASE STUDY.md: 10 focused PR links, K

name: Generated graph artifacts use compact references
summary: Kage generated graph artifacts should avoid duplicating canonical structural data. code graph/graph.json is a compact compatibility artifact that references structural files/symbols/imports, graph/graph.json references s

name: Generated repo-map and change-memory packets need structured context
summary: Fact: Kage generated repo map packets and branch change memory packets should include structured engineering context, not just prose. Why: audit structured memory coverage should stay at 100 without requiring manual edit

name: GitHub Pages publishes the viewer shell
summary: GitHub Pages should publish the static Kage viewer alongside the launch page. The Pages workflow copies mcp/viewer into docs/viewer during deployment, triggers on mcp/viewer changes, and the README/launch page link to ht

name: Graph freshness is content based
summary: Kage graph freshness uses input hashes for source files, approved memory packets, and code index inputs instead of requiring repo state.head to match HEAD. Push only operations and empty/same tree commits should not trig

name: Hosted viewer loads published Kage repo graph first
summary: The GitHub Pages viewer should load Kage's published repo graph/code graph/metrics from viewer/data/kage by default. The Pages workflow copies .agent memory/graph/graph.json, .agent memory/code graph/graph.json, and .age

name: Hosted viewer must auto-load a demo graph
summary: A hosted GitHub Pages viewer with no default graph is not useful. The viewer now loads mcp/viewer/demo/graph.json and demo/metrics.json when opened without graph/code URL params, so https://kage core.github.io/Kage/viewe

name: Init uses packet-only bootstrap; refresh builds full graphs
summary: kage init should prioritize fast first use: install policy/settings, migrate or generate repo memory packets, write lightweight packet indexes, and use packet only recall for the preview. It must not build the full knowl

name: Kage 1.1.8 adds stale memory GC
summary: Kage 1.1.8 adds kage gc for stale memory cleanup: dry-run preview, exact-file deprecation, forced delete for low-signal stale packets, and artifact refresh.

name: Launch site includes actual viewer demo video
summary: The launch site now includes an actual wide demo recording of the hosted Kage viewer at docs/assets/kage viewer demo.mp4, with poster docs/assets/kage viewer demo poster.png. The recording is generated by scripts/render

name: Launch site uses kage-core.com custom domain
summary: Kage launch site custom domain is kage core.com. GitHub Pages needs docs/CNAME with kage core.com, and DNS should point the apex domain to GitHub Pages records. After DNS propagates, enable Enforce HTTPS in repository Pa

name: Launch website eye logo glows and blinks
summary: The launch website now uses a custom glowing boxed eye logo in docs/index.html with docs/assets/kage eye.svg. The box frame should stay; the eye glow is stronger and blink animation is implemented with lightweight CSS sh

name: License changes to GPL-3.0-only for future releases
summary: Kage is switching future releases to GPL 3.0 only. GPL allows commercial use and selling, but distributed modified versions/derivatives must keep source available under GPL. README and npm metadata should say GPL 3.0 onl

name: Memory admission includes rationale, issues, and code explanations
summary: Fact: Kage memory admission must not use "would this change what a future agent does" as the only gate. Source backed rationale, bug causes, issue state, decisions, and non obvious code explanations are durable memory ev

name: Quick code indexing defers source files instead of silently skipping them
summary: Quick code indexing may use per run limits, but source files over quick file size or file count limits must be recorded as deferred in .agent memory/code graph/index manifest.json, not silently skipped. Metrics should ex

name: Read-only commands reuse current graph artifacts
summary: Kage read only commands should load current code and knowledge graph artifacts, keep an in process graph cache for MCP sessions, and avoid rewriting indexes unless inputs are missing or stale. Recall should precompute gr

name: README hero uses animated demo GIF
summary: The README hero should use the animated docs/assets/kage demo.gif, not the static kage viewer.svg. The GIF demonstrates the actual Kage install/recall/review workflow and matches the launch promise better than a static t

name: README is concise and viewer-first
summary: The root README was reduced from a long text heavy launch document to a concise product README that leads with the Kage Memory Terminal viewer, quick install, Codex/Claude setup, core commands, memory model, proof metric

name: README should stay user-first and concise
summary: The root and npm package READMEs were rewritten to be user first: value proposition, quick start, daily workflow, storage model, viewer, performance summary, trust model, and development only. Avoid reintroducing long re

name: Real team demo uses Codex CLI and Claude CLI proof logs
summary: The YC/team handoff demo should use a real two agent proof path: run Codex CLI against a temp repo to recall Kage memory, run Claude CLI against the same repo to recall the same packet, then render a wide demo video from

name: Recall ranking uses BM25 and viewer coalesces memory-code nodes
summary: Fact: Kage recall now uses BM25 as the lexical ranking stage and sorts repo recall by final hybrid score instead of raw keyword presence. The viewer also coalesces memory graph code entities with code graph nodes through

name: Refresh full mode bypasses unchanged graph reuse
summary: Kage refresh defaults to reusing a persisted code graph when the selected source stat fingerprint is unchanged, but maintainers can run kage refresh full or MCP kage refresh with full: true to intentionally bypass unchan

name: Refresh reuses graph builds and stays lightweight
summary: Refresh should reuse the code and knowledge graph objects produced during indexing and report lightweight metrics only. Full quality and benchmark calculations belong in the explicit metrics or benchmark commands because

name: Refresh reuses unchanged code graph by stat fingerprint
summary: Kage refresh should persist a cheap stat fingerprint for selected code graph inputs and return the current code graph when that fingerprint is unchanged. This prevents repeated refresh on large unchanged repos from rerea

name: Release 1.1.12 npm docs and launch notes
summary: Release 1.1.12 is the npm documentation and launch note patch. It adds an explicit Latest Release section to mcp/README.md so npm displays release notes, updates CHANGELOG.md for 1.1.11/1.1.12, and keeps the root README

name: Release 1.1.14 BM25 and memory-code graph package
summary: Release 1.1.14 publishes the BM25 recall and memory code graph trust pass. It includes vectorless BM25 lexical ranking with intent boosts, graph registry/audit/inbox/code index surfaces, viewer coalescing for memory grap

name: Release 1.1.15 adds guarded npm release flow
summary: Release 1.1.15 adds a guarded npm release helper and fixes memory only diff proposals. The release helper defaults to dry run unless publish is explicit, uses GIT EDITOR=true for git steps, fetches the current remote bra

name: Release 1.1.17 content-based graph freshness
summary: Release 1.1.17 publishes content based graph freshness for kage pr check. Graph artifacts use input hashes for source/config files, approved memory packets, and code index inputs, so push only operations and empty/same t

name: Release 1.1.3 memory lifecycle
summary: Release 1.1.3 adds production memory lifecycle commands: kage refresh rebuilds indexes/code graph/memory graph/metrics and marks stale memory from status, feedback, TTL, or path drift; kage pr summarize writes branch cha

name: Release 1.1.4 ambient policy and Claude hooks
summary: Release 1.1.4 updates the ambient agent policy and Claude Code hooks so Codex/Claude are instructed to run kage refresh after meaningful file changes, kage pr summarize or kage propose from diff before final handoff, and

name: Release 1.1.5 same-task A/B benchmark
summary: Release 1.1.5 adds same task A/B benchmarking. kage benchmark compare task compares baseline manual repo discovery against Kage recall plus code graph on the same repo/task, reporting estimated tokens saved, context redu

name: Release 1.1.7 launch readiness alignment
summary: Release 1.1.7 ships the launch ready kage context package alignment. It publishes the combined kage context MCP entrypoint, aligns README and MCP docs around it, updates the MCP server version, adds MCP tests for kage co

name: Release helper stays maintainer-only
summary: The npm release helper is source repo maintainer tooling, not runtime package functionality. Keep public package metadata free of release:npm scripts, and exclude dist/release.js from the npm tarball. Maintainers can bui

name: Social demo targets human re-onboarding frustration
summary: The Kage social demo should use doodle characters to show the human repeating repo-context bedtime stories to fresh AI agents until Kage makes the repo remember and the agent starts working on its own.

name: Structural index is canonical for code graph facts
summary: Kage code graph generation should use the structural index as the canonical source for files, symbols, and imports. buildCodeGraph should no longer run the old capped per file extraction path for those facts; it should d

name: Structural index is the only active code graph file selector
summary: Kage no longer keeps the old quick code graph file selection/cache path active. codeIndexSelection, KAGE MAX CODE GRAPH FILES, KAGE MAX CODE GRAPH SYMBOLS, codeGraphStatFingerprint, and the old code graph/file cache impl

name: Structural index uses parallel workers on large repos
summary: Kage structural index should parallelize per file structural extraction with worker threads once the file count crosses the configured threshold. Keep the public buildStructuralIndex API synchronous for refresh/index/MCP

name: Viewer combined mode must balance memory and code
summary: When loading a repo with a large code graph, Combined + High signal can appear to show only code because thousands of code symbols/routes compete with a small number of memory packets. The viewer should reserve part of t

name: Viewer demo must show visible state changes
summary: The viewer demo should not only caption actions such as searching runbook or switching Memory/Code views; it must show the viewer state change. scripts/render viewer demo.mjs now records the control action, returns to th

name: Viewer demo recording uses scripted product story
summary: The Kage viewer demo should be scripted before recording. The current storyboard is documented in docs/VIEWER DEMO SCRIPT.md and the renderer injects captions/highlights into the deployed GitHub Pages viewer while record

name: Viewer launch demo is click-only graph inspection
summary: The launch viewer demo should avoid search/dropdown flows. The stronger product demo is a click only walkthrough of the interactive graph: fit the graph, click a useful runbook memory node, click a path relation node, cl

name: Viewer opens structural code graph mode by default
summary: The local viewer should open kage viewer sessions in Code mode with an explicit structural code graph label, because Combined mode makes the optimized structural code graph look like the old memory heavy graph. Memory an

name: Website GitHub nav opens repo directly
summary: GitHub cannot be silently starred from a static website link; the launch site should use one GitHub nav button that opens the repo directly.

name: YC demo video shows the full Codex-to-Kage memory loop
summary: The YC application demo should be a 72-second silent 16:9 MP4 showing the full product loop: Codex calls Kage context, recalls repo memory, uses code graph facts, captures a repo-local memory packet, refreshes the graph, and shows inspectable memory in the viewer.

name: YC handoff demo needs CLI-first recording proof
summary: For the YC/team memory demo, a browser rendered product explainer is not enough when the ask is to record Codex and Claude CLI usage. Keep a CLI first artifact that shows terminal prompts, Codex output, Kage recall/code

name: Gotcha: init must bound generated code graph size
summary: Kage init on larger repos can become unusable if code graph generation indexes huge source blobs or unbounded JS/TS call edges. Keep first run indexing bounded: skip large source files, ignore generated/vendor/cache dire

name: OSS dogfood found Kage edge cases on vercel ai
summary: Dogfooding Kage on vercel/ai found three product gaps: root init/recall can fail on broken symlinks because README.md is a symlink whose target includes a newline; package level propose/pr check can generate root relativ

name: README hero image must avoid long terminal labels
summary: README hero image is rendered small on GitHub, so long terminal strings and inspector titles overflow or become unreadable. Keep the launch image closer to the earlier kage demo.gif composition: short step labels, compac

name: Release workflow should be non-interactive and preflight remote state
summary: During the 1.1.14 npm release, git push was fast but the workflow slowed down because origin/master moved after publish, git rebase continue opened vi for .git/COMMIT EDITMSG, and helpful feedback recording after the rel

name: Sub-agents spawned via Agent() tool don't receive SessionStart hooks
summary: Claude Code's SessionStart hook only fires when a human starts a session directly. Sub agents spawned programmatically via the Agent tool start with a fresh context and don't go through the hook pipeline. CLAUDE.md and a

name: Viewer performance fix must preserve graph visuals
summary: A viewer performance fix that changed the large graph into a striped/static overview was worse than the original. Keep the terminal canvas graph visual semantics intact; optimize responsiveness through cached edge/degree

name: Viewer root must redirect to parameterized /viewer/index.html
summary: The local viewer cannot serve index.html directly at / or /viewer because relative assets like ./app.js resolve to /app.js and 404. Redirect bare viewer routes to /viewer/index.html with the generated graph/code/metrics

name: Reference: AgentMemory viewer uses canvas force graph
summary: AgentMemory's real time viewer graph is a single file HTML/JS canvas implementation, not D3/Cytoscape. It loads graph data through REST endpoints graph/query and graph/stats, initializes nodes in a circle, runs a custom

name: Kage repo overview
summary: README excerpt: <div align="center" Kage Shared repo memory for AI coding agents Kage gives Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and other MCP agents the repo context they keep forgetting: commands, decisions, bugs, conventions,

name: Kage repo structure
summary: Detected repo structure: README.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/workflows, mcp. CI workflows: .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/claude.yml, .github/workflows/kage pr.yml, .github/workflows/kage sync.yml, .git

name: Claude Code MCP setup: ~/.claude.json + alwaysLoad + SessionStart hook
summary: Claude Code reads MCP servers from ~/.claude.json not ~/.claude/settings.json . The server entry requires type: "stdio" and alwaysLoad: true to make tools immediately visible without ToolSearch. A SessionStart hook at ~/

name: GitHub Actions: kage-pr.yml and kage-sync.yml automation
summary: Two GitHub Actions workflows automate kage memory maintenance. kage pr.yml fires on every PR — builds kage CLI, runs quality + pr check, posts a memory health comment via gh. kage sync.yml fires on every merge to master

name: kage_context is the single session-start tool replacing 4 separate calls
summary: kage context project dir, query replaces the old 4 step validate + recall + code graph + graph sequence. It runs all four in one call and returns a combined context block. Added in mcp/index.ts callTool handler and liste
